info.title: 1Platform API
info.description:
# 1Platform API

REST API powering the **1Platform** platform — content generation, SEO tooling, billing, invoicing, and third-party integrations.

---

## Authentication

1Platform uses a **two-token** authentication model:

| Token | Header | How to obtain | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------------|---------|
| **App token** | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` | `POST /api/v1/auth/token` with your App API key | Identifies the calling application |
| **User token** | `x-user-token: <token>` | `POST /api/v1/users/token` with the user API key | Identifies the end user |

Most endpoints require **both** tokens. Exceptions are noted per endpoint.

## Quickstart

```bash
# 1. Get an app token
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/auth/token \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"apiKey": "ak-your-app-api-key"}'

# 2. Get a user token (requires app token)
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users/token \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"apiKey": "sk-user-api-key"}'

# 3. Call any protected endpoint (requires both tokens)
curl https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users/profile \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN"
```

## Base URL

All endpoints are prefixed with `/api/v1/`.

## Rate Limits

| Category | Limit |
|----------|-------|
| Health | 100 req/min |
| Auth | 10 req/min |
| Read operations | 50 req/min |
| Write operations | 20 req/min |
| Keyword generation | 10 req/min |
| Content generation | 5 req/min |
| Indexing | 20 req/min |
| Agent runs | 10 req/min |
| Agent wizard | 20 req/min |
| Agent CRUD | 30 req/min |
| Agent metrics | 10 req/min |
| Client logs | 10 req/min |
| Analytics reads | 30 req/min |
| Analytics writes | 10 req/min |
| Analytics reports | 20 req/min |
| Analytics realtime | 10 req/min |
| Analytics events | 10 req/min |

## Response Format

All endpoints return a standard wrapper (except webhooks):

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { ... },
  "msg": "Operation completed successfully"
}
```

get.description:
Returns the current health status of the API and its dependencies (database connectivity, etc.).

- **Auth:** None required
- **Rate limit:** 100 req/min

post.description:
Authenticate with an App API key and receive a JWT app token.

- **Auth:** None — this is the entry point for obtaining tokens
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min
- The returned `access_token` must be sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all protected endpoints
- Token expires after the time indicated in `expires_in` (seconds)

Analyze a domain and extract SEO keywords using AI-powered search analysis.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min
- Requires an active App with AI provider configured

**Process:**
1. Analyzes domain content and structure
2. Extracts relevant keywords via search providers
3. Uses 1Platform AI to detect keyword cannibalization
4. Categorizes keywords by search intent and volume
5. Returns structured keyword data with metrics

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/posts/keywords/ \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"domain": "example.com", "country": "es", "lang": "en"}'
```

401.description: Authentication failed — invalid or missing token

500.description: Service configuration error or provider unavailable

post.description:
Discover SEO keywords for a topic/theme using Google search analysis.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min

**Process:**
1. Searches topic in Google to discover top niche domains
2. Analyzes each domain for topic-relevant pages
3. Extracts main keyword from each page title via AI
4. Removes semantic duplicates (cannibalization)
5. Returns structured keyword data with estimated volume

401.description: Authentication failed — invalid or missing token

post.description:
Submit an async content generation job for a target keyword.

Returns immediately with a job ID. Use `GET /content/jobs/{job_id}` to poll status.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 5 req/min (AI-intensive)
- Requires AI and image provider keys in the App configuration

**Process (runs in background):**
1. Generates title variations via 1Platform AI
2. Searches related content using search providers
3. Produces structured article with headings and paragraphs
4. Fetches relevant images from the configured image library
5. Creates a URL-friendly slug
6. Classifies content into provided categories

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/posts/content/ \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"keyword": "best running shoes", "lang": "en", "image_provider": "default", "categories": ["sports"]}'
```

example.msg: Content generation job created for keyword: best running shoes

401.description: Authentication failed — invalid or missing token

500.description: Service configuration error or AI provider unavailable

get.description:
Poll the status of an async content generation job.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- Returns job status: `pending`, `processing`, `completed`, or `failed`
- On completion, includes the full content generation result in `data.result`
- On failure, includes error details in `data.error`

**Recommended polling interval:** 5 seconds

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/posts/content/jobs/JOB_ID \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN"
```

401.description: Authentication failed — invalid or missing token

post.description:
Submit a URL to the Google Indexing API for faster crawling.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- The URL must belong to a website verified in Google Search Console
- A service account with Search Console access is required
- The URL must be publicly accessible

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/posts/indexing/ \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"url": "https://example.com/new-page"}'
```

400.description: Invalid URL format or inaccessible URL

401.description: Authentication failed — invalid or missing token

500.description: Google Indexing API error or service unavailable

post.description:
Authenticate with a user API key and receive a JWT user token.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` only (no user token needed)
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min
- The returned token must be sent as the `x-user-token` header on all user-scoped requests
- Token expires after the time indicated in `expires_in` (seconds)

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users/token \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"apiKey": "sk-user-api-key"}'
```

Register a new user with automatic billing and subscription setup.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` only (no user token needed)
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Creates a User, Billing (balance=0 USD), and Subscription (Free Plan)
- Returns the user's `api_key` used to obtain user tokens via `POST /api/v1/users/token`

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "user@example.com"}'
```

201.description: User created successfully with billing and subscription

example.msg: User created successfully with billing and subscription

400.description: Invalid email format or validation error

get.description:
Retrieve the authenticated user's profile information.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- Returns id, username, email, is_active, and created_at

Retrieve billing balance and active subscription data for the authenticated user.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- Returns balance, currency, and active subscription details

Retrieve available website categories for content classification.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- Filter by status: `active` (default), `inactive`, or `all`

parameters.name: status_filter

schema.description: Filter by status: active, inactive, or all
schema.title: Status Filter

parameters.description: Filter by status: active, inactive, or all

post.description:
Register a new website for the authenticated user.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Validates URL uniqueness per user
- Optionally enriches the website with an AI-generated description

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users/websites \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"url": "https://myblog.com", "category_id": "<objectId>", "lang": "es"}'
```

409.description: Website already exists for this user

get.description:
Search for a website by its URL among the authenticated user's websites.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- Pass the URL as a query parameter: `?url=https://example.com`

patch.description:
Update an existing website for the authenticated user.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Only the website owner can update it
- All fields are optional (partial update)
- Use this endpoint to set or update the `category_id`

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X PATCH https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users/websites/<WEBSITE_ID> \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"category_id": "<objectId>", "site_name": "Updated Name"}'
```

get.description:
Retrieve details of a specific website by its ID.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- Only returns websites owned by the authenticated user

delete.description:
Remove a website from the authenticated user's account.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Only the website owner can delete it
- This action is irreversible

post.description:
Add or verify a website in the link-building marketplace.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- **Actions:** `add` (register website) or `verify` (confirm ownership)
- The user must have marketplace credentials configured
- After adding, verify ownership to activate the listing

400.description: Invalid action, missing credentials, or verification failed

post.description:
Add, verify, delete a website or manage sitemaps in Google Search Console.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- **Actions:** `add`, `verify`, `delete`, `sitemaps`
- The website must be registered in the platform before adding to Search Console
- For `sitemaps` action, include a `payload.sitemaps` array with sitemap URLs
- Requires a Google service account with Search Console access

example.msg: Website verified successfully in Search Console

400.description: Invalid action or website not registered in Search Console

post.summary: Generate legal pages for a website
post.description:
Generate all legal pages for a website: privacy policy, cookie policy, legal notice, contact page, and about-me page.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Template-based pages use static HTML with variable substitution
- Contact and about-me pages are generated via 1Platform AI
- Requires an active App with AI provider configured

Generate realistic fictional comments using AI for website content seeding.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 5 req/min
- Each comment includes a fictional author name and content text
- All comments are generated in a single AI request
- Requires an active App with AI provider configured

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users/generations/comments \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"count": 5, "lang": "es", "context": "Blog de tecnología"}'
```

400.description: Invalid input or AI generation failed

post.description:
Generate images using a configurable AI provider.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 5 req/min
- Each image is generated individually; partial results are returned if some attempts fail
- Image metadata is persisted for each generation
- Requires an active App with AI provider configured

200.description: Images generated successfully (may include partial results)

post.description:
Generate a complete profile card for a person using AI.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 5 req/min
- Includes gender detection, bio generation, social media discovery, and profile image retrieval
- Provide the person's name as `owner`; optional `context` enriches the bio
- Requires AI, search, and image provider keys in the App configuration

Revoke all existing JWT tokens for the authenticated user.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min
- Increments the user's `token_version`, invalidating all previously issued tokens
- After calling this endpoint, the user must obtain a new token via `POST /api/v1/users/token`

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users/revoke-tokens \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN"
```

Create a new payment transaction and receive a checkout URL.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Payment is processed via the integrated payment service
- Returns a `payment_url` where the user completes checkout
- Transaction starts with status `CREATED`; status updates arrive via webhook

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.1platform.pro/api/v1/users/transactions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $APP_TOKEN" \
  -H "x-user-token: $USER_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"amount": 25.00, "currency": "USD", "description": "Credit purchase"}'
```

201.description: Transaction created — redirect user to payment URL

example.msg: Transaction created successfully. Please complete payment at the provided URL.

404.description: Billing record not found for user

get.description:
Retrieve paginated transaction history for the authenticated user.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- `limit`: max transactions to return (default: 50, max: 100)
- `skip`: offset for pagination (default: 0)

parameters.name: skip

schema.description: Number of transactions to skip for pagination
schema.title: Skip

parameters.description: Number of transactions to skip for pagination

get.description:
Retrieve details of a specific subscription for the authenticated user.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- **Note:** This endpoint currently returns a preview response — full subscription management will be available in a future release

post.description:
Receive transaction status updates from the payment service.

- **Auth:** None — this endpoint is called by the external payment provider
- **Idempotent:** Duplicate webhooks are safely ignored (prevents double-charging)
- **Always returns HTTP 200** to prevent aggressive retries from the provider

- Webhook authenticity is verified via HMAC signature validation
**Flow:**
1. Extract `customKeys.externalId` to find the local transaction
2. Check idempotency (if status unchanged, return success)
3. Validate status transition
4. Update transaction status
5. If approved, credit the user's balance

**Response format** (simple JSON, not ResponseWrapper):
```json
{"status": "success", "message": "Processed"}
```

status.title: Status
status.description: Transaction status (will be mapped to TransactionStatus enum)

customKeys.title: Customkeys
customKeys.description: Custom keys sent during transaction creation

schema.title: WebhookPayload
schema.description:
Webhook payload from payment service.

Used by POST /api/v1/webhooks/transactions endpoint.
Field names match payment service's webhook format.

200.description: Webhook processed (or safely ignored if duplicate)

post.description:
Register a new business entity for invoicing.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- The business is used as the issuing party on invoices
- Business identifiers (tax ID) must be unique
- Optionally set `commerceId` for electronic invoicing (FEL) integration

get.description:
Retrieve details of a specific business.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min

post.description:
Create a new branch for a business.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- The authenticated user is automatically assigned to the new branch
- Each branch can issue invoices independently

get.description:
Retrieve details of a specific branch within a business.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min

400.description: Branch does not belong to this business

post.description:
Issue a new invoice for a business via electronic invoicing (FEL).

- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
**Flow:**
1. Authenticate and resolve businessId from the path
2. Validate business exists
3. Validate invoice items and receptorInfo
4. Persist invoice associated to business_id
5. If `emitOnCreate=true`, emit via FEL using the business NIT

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- The invoice is associated directly to the business (via business_id)
- The business NIT is used as the emitter for FEL invoicing

201.description: Invoice created and registered via FEL

422.description: Validation error or FEL invoicing error

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of invoices for a business.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- Supports filtering by status and date range
- Supports sorting by any field (default: `created_at` desc)
- Invoices are scoped to the business's commerceId

parameters.name: date_from

schema.description: Filter invoices created on or after this date (ISO 8601)
schema.title: Date From

parameters.description: Filter invoices created on or after this date (ISO 8601)

parameters.name: date_to

schema.description: Filter invoices created on or before this date (ISO 8601)
schema.title: Date To

parameters.description: Filter invoices created on or before this date (ISO 8601)

get.description:
Retrieve full details of a specific invoice.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- The invoice must belong to the specified business
- FEL report metadata is automatically fetched and cached on first access

delete.description:
Cancel an existing invoice.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>` + `x-user-token: <user_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- The invoice must belong to the specified business
- A cancellation reason is required in the request body
- Already-cancelled invoices cannot be cancelled again
- If the invoice was emitted, it will be voided via electronic invoicing (FEL)

get.description:
Retrieve paginated API logs for the authenticated app.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Optional:** `x-user-token` — when present, returns only logs for that user
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min
- Supports filtering by website, provider, status, error type, etc.

parameters.name: to_date

schema.description: Filter logs up to this date
schema.title: To Date

parameters.description: Filter logs up to this date

delete.description:
Delete API logs matching the given filters.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- An audit log entry is created before deletion

parameters.name: confirm

schema.description: Must be set to true to confirm bulk deletion
schema.title: Confirm

parameters.description: Must be set to true to confirm bulk deletion

get.description:
Retrieve full details of a specific API log entry.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 50 req/min

parameters.name: log_id

schema.description: Unique identifier of the log entry
schema.title: Log Id

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the log entry

post.description:
Ingest a batch of client-side log entries.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min
- Maximum 50 entries per batch
- Timestamps must be within 7 days in the past and 5 minutes in the future

get.description:
Return the full agent template catalog.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

get.summary: Get a catalog template by slug
get.description:
Return a single agent template from the catalog.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

post.description:
Start a new guided wizard session for agent creation.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Requires AI API key configured

Continue an active wizard session with a user reply.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Requires AI API key configured

Confirm a wizard session and create the agent from the generated configuration.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min

404.description: Session not found or no config

get.description:
Retrieve an existing wizard session by ID.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

post.description:
Create a new agent definition.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min
- Maximum 50 agents per app
- Cron schedules must have at least 15-minute intervals

429.description: Rate limit exceeded or quota exceeded

get.description:
List agents for the authenticated app with pagination.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

Return aggregated operational metrics for the agent domain.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min
- Admin-level visibility into platform capacity and usage

Retrieve an agent definition by ID.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

put.description:
Update an existing agent definition. All fields are optional.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

delete.description:
Delete an agent definition.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

post.description:
Trigger a new agent run. Returns immediately with the run in PENDING status.
The run executes asynchronously in the background.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min
- Requires AI API key configured
- Agent must be active
- Subject to per-agent and global concurrency limits

503.description: Agent inactive or global capacity full

get.description:
List execution runs for a specific agent with optional filters.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min
- Filter by status: pending, running, completed, failed
- Filter by trigger: manual, scheduled

Retrieve details of a specific agent run.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

post.description:
Stop a running or pending agent run.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 10 req/min
- Only runs in PENDING or RUNNING status can be stopped

409.description: Run cannot be stopped in current status

get.description:
List human-in-the-loop actions for the authenticated app.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min
- Filter by type, status, or agent_id

Retrieve a specific agent action by ID.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 30 req/min

patch.description:
Consume a pending human-in-the-loop action.

- **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <app_token>`
- **Rate limit:** 20 req/min
- Action must be in PENDING status

post.description: Provision a new analytics property for a website. The provisioning runs in the background — poll GET /status for completion. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 10/minute.

example.msg: Analytics provisioning started. Poll GET /status for completion.

409.description: Analytics already active for this website

429.description: Rate limit exceeded or provisioning cooldown active

delete.description: Disconnect the analytics property from a website without deleting it. The property can be reconnected later. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 10/minute.
Permanently deprovision the analytics property for a website. Requires confirm=true to proceed. This action cannot be undone. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 10/minute.

get.description: Retrieve the current analytics provisioning status for a website. Returns property details, connection state, and any error messages. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 30/minute.
List all analytics accounts accessible to the authenticated user. Used to select an account during property provisioning. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 30/minute.

Retrieve detailed information about the analytics property linked to a website. Includes timezone, currency, service level, and creation time. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 30/minute.
List all data streams configured for the analytics property. Each stream corresponds to a web data source. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 30/minute.
Retrieve the JavaScript tag snippet for embedding analytics tracking on a website. The tag must be placed in the HTML head section. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 30/minute.
List all custom dimensions configured for the analytics property. Custom dimensions track additional attributes on events. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 30/minute.
List all key events (conversions) configured for the analytics property. Key events track important user actions on the website. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 30/minute.

Retrieve aggregated site metrics including sessions, users, pageviews, bounce rate, and top pages/sources for a given period with optional comparison. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 20/minute.
Retrieve real-time analytics data including active users, top pages, traffic sources, and countries for the last 30 minutes. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 10/minute.
Retrieve analytics metrics for a specific page path on the website. Returns views, unique views, average time on page, and bounce rate. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 20/minute.

post.description: Send a server-side event via the Measurement Protocol. Useful for tracking conversions and events that happen outside the browser. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 10/minute.

get.description: Generate an OAuth consent URL with PKCE for connecting analytics. The returned URL must be opened in a browser popup. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 10/minute.

example.msg: Open this URL to authorize analytics

post.description: Revoke the OAuth tokens for a website's analytics connection. The website will need to re-authorize to reconnect analytics. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 10/minute.

404.description: OAuth not configured for this website

get.description: Check whether OAuth is connected for a website's analytics integration. Returns connection state, authorized email, and authorization timestamp. Requires both app and user tokens. Rate limit: 30/minute.
Handles the OAuth redirect after user authorization. Exchanges the authorization code for tokens and stores them. Returns an HTML page that sends a postMessage to the opener window.

TransactionStatus.title: TransactionStatus
TransactionStatus.description:
Transaction status enum matching payment service statuses.

State machine:
- CREATED (initial) can transition to any terminal state
- Terminal states (APPROVED, DENIED, etc.) cannot transition
- Idempotent: same status → same status is allowed

tags.name: API Status
tags.description: Check if the API and its dependencies are running. No authentication required.

tags.name: Get App Token
tags.description: Obtain a JWT app token using your App API key. The returned token must be sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all protected endpoints.

tags.name: Keywords, Content & Indexing
tags.description: Extract SEO keywords from a domain, generate AI-powered articles, and submit URLs to Google for indexing.

tags.name: Users & Tokens
tags.description: Create user accounts and obtain user JWT tokens. New users are automatically set up with billing and a subscription plan.

tags.name: My Profile
tags.description: View the authenticated user's profile information (username, email, status).

tags.name: Balance & Billing
tags.description: Check your current balance, currency, and active subscription details.

tags.name: Content Categories
tags.description: Browse available categories used to classify websites and generated content.

tags.name: Websites
tags.description: Add, update, search, and delete websites for the authenticated user. Also generate legal pages (privacy policy, cookie policy, legal notice).

tags.name: Link Building & Search Console
tags.description: Register and verify websites in the link-building marketplace, and manage Google Search Console properties and sitemaps.

tags.name: AI Generators
tags.description: Generate AI content on demand: fictional comments for blogs, images from text prompts, and complete profile cards with bio and avatar.

tags.name: Payments
tags.description: Create payment transactions, get a checkout URL, and retrieve your full transaction history.

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: View subscription plan details and current plan status.

tags.name: Payment Notifications
tags.description: Receive automatic payment status updates from the payment service. No authentication required — designed for server-to-server callbacks. All responses return HTTP 200 to prevent retries.

tags.name: Businesses & Branches
tags.description: Register business entities and their branches for electronic invoicing. Each branch can issue invoices independently.

tags.name: Electronic Invoicing (FEL)
tags.description: Create, list, view, and cancel electronic invoices (FEL Guatemala). Invoices are linked to a business and emitted using its tax ID (NIT).

tags.name: Activity Logs
tags.description: View, filter, and clear API activity logs. Also ingest client-side log entries for centralized observability.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Analytics integration — connect, provision properties, track content performance, and query real-time metrics.

tags.name: Agents - Catalog
tags.description: Browse the agent template catalog to discover pre-built automation recipes.

tags.name: Agents - Wizard
tags.description: Create agents interactively using a guided wizard powered by AI. Start a session, answer questions, and confirm to deploy.

tags.name: Agents - CRUD
tags.description: Create, list, read, update, and delete agent definitions.

tags.name: Agents - Execution
tags.description: Trigger agent runs, list execution history, inspect run details, and stop running or pending executions.

tags.name: Agents - Metrics
tags.description: View aggregated operational metrics for the agent platform (admin-level).

tags.name: Agents - Actions
tags.description: List, inspect, and consume human-in-the-loop actions generated by agent runs. Actions require manual approval before the agent proceeds.
